Alright, welcome back. In this lesson, we are going to be talking about how enrollment and how an empowered decision is not something to strive for in of itself, but actually comes as an emergent after a few other milestones have been met. So that's really at the crux of what we mean by coaching the conversion, right? Coaching all these milestones along the way. And we're gonna make this really practical in terms of what we are coaching on an enrollment call. So, redux for coaching the conversion, if you're not familiar yet, the CTC framework is built on the underlying premise that a conversion is nothing but the transformation between point A where someone is at mentally, emotionally, belief-wise around the problem you're solving before they came into your sphere of influence, whether that's a funnel, whether that's a sales page, whether that's an email, or in this case on a sales call and point buyer who they have to become at the end of that ecosystem or that container in order to be empowered to say yes. And it's the job of our marketing and sales to empower that transformation by coaching the right beliefs, mindset shifts, identity shifts, and epiphanies to facilitate it. So the sale is not something we strive for in and of itself. It's actually a natural emergent when this series of precursors, microconversions, milestones, states, or conditions have been satisfied. The metaphor I've been using for years on this is you don't knock down a 20 ton domino with sheer blunt force, right? This is the world and domain of persuasion, of urgency, of fear mongering, of sales driven gas lighting, all these elements that we are dismantling that actually come from a place of insecurity, right? Because they haven't been able to naturally lead that transformation. It all comes down to them pushing, pushing, pushing to try to knock down that 20 ton domino. So instead of doing that, you knock over the smaller ones or you allow them to gently fall on their own accord and let momentum naturally knock down the final one to complete the process. So 10x launches coaches the conversion across a launch or an email funnel, which tends to be more of a horizontal, horizontal container that plays out over several assets over several days. 10x sales pages flips that on a TED, right? And coaches it vertically on a single asset, automated intimacy, coaches the conversion more dynamically across many different assets within your marketing funnel or your ecosystem. And CTC enrollment where we are right now coaches the conversion in the most intimate dynamic, powerful container of all, which is your enrollment conversations. So the reframe we start with is you don't have leads that need to be closed. You have pre customers in need of transformation on identity, on belief and their ability to act and the conditions of safety of power of vision that drive that transformation. And in this sacred 20 to 50 minute container that you're going to have with them, it's your duty, it's your responsibility to do your best to coach it. So leads versus pre customers. This isn't just linguistics, linguistics or semantics. This is how I view anyone that I'm in conversation with, we fully acknowledge that every single person who enters your sphere of influence and who has chosen to be in your sphere of influence has the ability and potential to become a customer that there is this latent potential. And it's up to you as the conversion coach to do what you can ethically to unlock it, to understand what they need and to facilitate and empower that transformation. So that is the role of the conversion coach on an enrollment call. Point to consider once again, we spoke about this in the last lesson, the observer effect when you treat leads as if they are high value customers with the same level of respect and honoring and reverence and appreciation, something magically happens. They feel valued and they start reorienting around how you view and experience them. So how would you show up differently to your next call? If you believed that everyone you're engaging with will inevitably become a customer, right? How would that shift and change the way you show up and interact? Would you be more loose and allowing for that to play out however it needs to play out and more resourceful and attuned to what you could do to coach that transformation? That's typically where we want to land on this. We want to come at it from this place where we know that potential exists. They will inevitably get there and we can therefore surrender to being present on the call and delivering what we need to do to facilitate that transformation. So when you embrace this, it's no longer about selling. It's about your pre-customer experiencing a transformation within your sales process, not just of belief, but identity of self-identity of how they identify with a new potential reality as more real, as more likely, as more vivid, as more lucid than the alternate reality that they're trying to kind of escape from. So this is what makes your offer a natural, frictionless pathway. But most importantly, as a world-class coach or educator, not as a high pressure, high ticket closer, but as someone who is here to lead, to serve, to educate, to coach, why would you wait until money changes hands before leveraging your greatest gift? Why would you turn yourself into something you're not and try to adopt the frame and adopt the beingness and adopt the skills of a high ticket closer and all that pressure that never really resonated or fit well to begin with when you have all the skills, abilities and capacities necessary and the disposition necessary to leverage your greatest gift where it can actually come online in the greatest way. So five key principles of coaching the conversion on sales calls acknowledge that there is a version of your prospect that naturally becomes an empowered customer and that a sale happens naturally when certain states, understandings and identities are met. And that is your job as the conversion coach to empower and facilitate that transformation. And the speed of which this transformation is achieved is quantum, right? How fast it's achieved is not linear. It can crack in a single word in a single realization or it may not crack at all. This is the territory of epiphanies of tipping points of aha moments where something just clicks and knowing this or an intuition that leads them into that transformation. And because this is not a linear process, our scripts, the way we lead these calls can't be formulaic. They can't be rigid. Otherwise, both us and our customers will feel stifled and straight jacketed in the process which demands something more flexible that gives space gives the opportunity for these tipping point tipping points and aha moments to actually occur. So that is what we are going to be working with with the CTC enrollment process. The next few lessons are going to go deeper in this. So for now, take a little breather and I'll meet you in that next lesson.
